title: 'Scream VI' and the Scream Movie Rankings
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins welcome back Chris Ryan and a new guest, Sean's sister and film-major-in-training Grace Fennessey, to review 'Scream VI' and rank all six films in the franchise, after a Nancy Meyers Netflix rom-com falls apart over a $150 million kitchen budget.

segments.heading: The news
segments.note: a canceled Nancy Meyers movie and 'Your Place or Mine'

films.title: Your Place or Mine
films.note: Netflix's Reese Witherspoon/Ashton Kutcher rom-com, savaged for its 'CGI Brooklyn Bridge,' a FaceTime-heavy setup that keeps its leads apart until the end despite living in the same story, and zero chemistry between the two leads; floated as a cautionary tale for why Netflix just walked away from a $150 million Nancy Meyers movie.

segments.heading: 'Scream VI'
segments.note: the goriest entry yet, but a familiar reveal

films.title: Scream VI
films.note: The 'core four' (Sam, Tara, Chad, and Mindy) relocate to New York and face Ghostface again, with Courteney Cox's Gale and Hayden Panettiere's Kirby folded back into the story. The hosts call it the most violent entry yet, singling out the subway and fire-escape-ladder sequences as series highlights, but knock Dermot Mulroney's villain reveal as telegraphed from his first scene and needle the movie's shaky grip on New York geography, from Hudson-to-uptown walks to a stolen cop car making it downtown in no time. Consensus: thumbs up.

segments.heading: Radio Silence's other movie, and an elevated-horror detour

films.title: Ready or Not
films.note: The Radio Silence duo's 2019 breakout, made before they inherited Scream, and where they first worked with Samara Weaving -- who cameos in 'Scream VI,' killed in its own opening sequence, as a nod to that earlier collaboration.

films.title: Skinamarink
films.note: An 80-minute, VHS-shot experimental horror film on Shudder, told almost entirely from the perspective of two small children trapped in a house with no doors or windows; brought up as a break from the 'elevated horror' wave.

films.title: The Quiet Girl
films.note: Grace caught the Irish drama in a theater and loved it; Sean jokingly floats it as a secret Scream prequel, with the lead girl growing up to become Sidney Prescott.

segments.heading: The Scream Movie Rankings
segments.note: 'Scream' at No. 1 was unanimous; the other five split hard by host
segments.subject: Scream

films.title: Scream
films.note: No. 1 for everyone. The unanimous, unassailable original.

films.title: Scream 2
films.note: Praised for the classroom sequel-theory scene, Kevin Williamson's 'fastball' meta writing, and Sarah Michelle Gellar's sorority-house kill; the hosts are cooler on its other kills.

films.title: Scream 4
films.note: Grace's sleeper favorite -- 'low-key one of my favorite Screams' -- for its opening and Emma Roberts's willingly hateable villain turn opposite Rory Culkin.

films.title: Scream (2022)
films.note: Warmly reviewed as a genuine surprise, anchored by Dewey's shocking death and strong Jack Quaid/Mikey Madison performances; Sean says he might like it more than most of the 'vintage' entries.

films.title: Scream VI
films.note: This episode's film -- see the review above.

films.title: Scream 3
films.note: The most divisive entry: Chris's personal favorite behind the original, for Parker Posey, the Cotton Weary limo kill, and the Woodsboro-replica-set finale; Grace ranks it dead last, unable to get past the Sidney's-mother-ghost subplot and a forgettable killer reveal.

films: Friday the 13th Part VIII: Jason Takes Manhattan

Unreleased/canceled: Quentin Tarantino's 'The Movie Critic' (unmade at time of taping)
